channel.title: Emergency Communications on Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club
channel.description: Recent content in Emergency Communications on Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club

item.title: EmComm Winlink Operations
item.description:
📡 Net Preamble & Overview

What is this Winlink Net?

  • This specialized training net allows our ARES / AuxComm volunteers to become highly familiar with Winlink data terminal configurations through regular, practical deployment.
  • It systematically trains our personnel on how Winlink can be utilized to route high-priority messages, ICS forms, and logistics manifests to assist regional agencies during an actual emergency, whether natural or man-made.

How Does the Net Operate?

  • Frequency & Cadence: Every two weeks, a brand-new training scenario or specialized exercise is assigned to our emergency communications roster.
  • Distribution: We distribute a technical PDF instruction layout detailing the parameters needed to successfully resolve the challenge. This packet goes out via email networks and our operational GroupMe pipeline.
  • Evaluation: The appointed Net Control Station (NCS) evaluates each volunteer’s submitted payload data message and routes back personalized performance feedback via a standard Winlink reply.
  • Mapping Tracker: The NCS records compliance metrics in the operations spreadsheet and generates a visual distribution grid map at the end of the strict exercise window to track situational coverage.

Who Can Participate in This Net?

Originally, this training framework was exclusively tailored for local Brunswick County ARES/AuxComm units. However, because we host our historic exercise logs on the public BSARC portal, any licensed Amateur Radio Operator is welcome to participate! Review the active instructions within the PDF sheets listed below, complete the field requirements, and route your final data packet to the designated Winlink NCS callsign specified inside that specific assignment packet.

item.title: Amateur Radio Emergency Service (ARES)
item.description:
Brunswick County ARRL ARES Program

The Amateur Radio Emergency Service (ARES) consists of licensed radio amateurs who have voluntarily registered their qualifications and equipment for auxiliary communications duties under the national ARRL leadership matrix.

Every licensed amateur, regardless of membership in the ARRL or any other local organization, is eligible to join. Our primary mission is securing emergency communications support loops for non-governmental welfare and humanitarian field units (such as the American Red Cross and local shelters) during community emergencies.

item.title: Auxiliary Communications (AUXCOMM)
item.description:
Brunswick County Government AUXCOMM Infrastructure

The Auxiliary Communications (AUXCOMM) unit is a formally recognized public safety component integrated directly into the Brunswick County Emergency Management Agency under the regulatory guidance of CISA and the Department of Homeland Security (DHS).

Unlike private organizational programs, AUXCOMM hams are formally registered auxiliary government volunteers mobilized under state directives to execute direct communications support inside local municipal assets—including Emergency Operations Centers (EOCs), mobile public safety command trailers, and municipal 911 dispatch centers during official county disaster declarations.

item.title: Skywarn Severe Weather Operations
item.description:
The Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club works in close synchronization with the National Weather Service (NWS) to provide critical ground-truth verification when severe or tropical weather systems threaten our region.

📡 Operational Procedures & Infrastructure

When hazardous conditions arise, the Brunswick County Skywarn Emergency Coordinators (ECs) will activate our localized spotter network. Spotters on the ground gather vital meteorological information and transmit reports directly to the ECs.
